articles.title: Australia's unemployment rate set to remain steady in March amid hawkish RBA outlook
articles.expand: Australia will publish the monthly employment report for March on Thursday at 01:30 GMT, and market participants expect a modest increase in job creation.

articles.title: AUD/USD climbs above 0.7170 as truce hopes lift risk appetite
articles.expand: The Australian Dollar extended its gains on Wednesday, up by 0.72% as risk appetite improved amid speculation of a de-escalation of the conflict, keeping oil prices in check as WTI held above $91, despite posting losses of nearly 0.80%. At the time of writing, the AUD/USD trades at 0.7173.

articles.title: ECB officials lean toward April rate hold amid Iran war uncertainty
articles.expand: ECB officials are leaning toward holding rates in April, opting to wait for clearer evidence on the Iran war’s impact, even as markets continue to price further rate hikes later this year, Bloomberg reports. Earlier: ECB’s Schnabel says bank can take time to assess Iran shock Summary: ECB policymakers are leaning toward holding rates at the April m...

articles.title: IMF says BOJ can look through Iran war inflation shock
articles.expand: IMF says the BOJ can look through Iran war-driven inflation, with limited second-round effects, allowing gradual rate hikes to continue as inflation still converges to the 2% target by 2027. Summary: The IMF said the Bank of Japan can “see through” inflation pressures from the Iran war, with limited second-round effects expected. IMF mission chief ...

articles.title: ICYMI - Fed’s Musalem says oil shock to keep core inflation near 3%
articles.expand: Fed’s Musalem said oil prices from the Iran war could keep core inflation near 3% this year, likely forcing the Fed to hold rates for longer as supply shocks lift costs and growth slows. Summary: St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem said high oil prices are likely to keep core inflation near 3% this year, above the Fed’s 2% target. The Fed is li...

articles.title: Fed’s Musalem: Easing tariff impact will help lower inflation
articles.expand: Alberto Musalem, President of the Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank of St. Louis, said in an interview with Reuters on Wednesday that the Oil shock caused by the Middle East war is likely feeding core inflation, and he expects it to be near 3% throughout the year.

articles.title: USD/JPY steady below 160.00 amid Japan intervention risks
articles.expand: USD/JPY holds firm on Wednesday despite a broadly weaker US Dollar (USD). The pair remains confined within a one-month trading range, as elevated Oil prices linked to tensions in the Middle East continue to weigh on the Japanese Yen (JPY).

articles.title: Fed's Beige Book: Overall activity showed slight to modest growth in 8 of 12 districts
articles.expand: Slight to modest growth in 8 of 12 districts Two districts with little change Two districts with modest declines The April Beige Book landed with a thud. Eight of twelve districts managed slight-to-modest growth, but two were flat and two — Boston and New York — actually contracted. That's not a recession signal, but it's not exactly confidence-ins...

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Getting ready for a challenge of 2026 highs?
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar keeps its robust recovery well in place, opening the door for AUD/USD to a potential challenge of the YTD peaks in levels just shy of 0.7200 the figure recorded in early March.

articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair halts its advance on Wednesday and remains steady around 1.3570 as optimism about a resumption of US-Iran talks has tempered. At the same time, US equities extended their gains, and the Greenback seems to have bottomed after hitting a six-week low. Read More...

articles.title: USD/TRY: Stable path with renewed carry interest – ING
articles.expand: ING analysts Muhammet Mercan, Frantisek Taborsky and James Wilson note that the Turkish Lira (TRY) has stabilised after US-Iran tensions eased, with the Central Bank of Turkey (CBT) maintaining market confidence and a steady USD/TRY path.

articles.title: ECB sources report: Policymakers wary of April rate hike
articles.expand: Reuters is out with an ECB sources report that says they're wary of hiking in April as there is no evidence yet of second-round effects. Yesterday, the market had been pricing in a 39% chance of a hike and this looks designed to drive that down. The way the ECB does business is bizarre as Lagarde was speaking yesterday and could have done this hers...

articles.title: ECB’s Nagel: As long as Hormuz is not resolved, the danger of higher inflation rises
articles.expand: Joachim Nagel, President of the Bundesbank and member of the European Central Bank (ECB) spoke in an interview with Bloomberg on Wednesday. He said that questions about the Strait of Hormuz are essential, as we're between the baseline and an adverse scenario.

articles.title: Eurozone: Downside risks for industry grow after war shock – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Bert Colijn notes that Eurozone industrial production rose just 0.4% in February versus January, leaving output below most of 2025 levels. He highlights that higher energy prices and the Middle East war are set to weigh further on energy‑intensive industry and investment.
